---
title: "Research Projects"
---

## Payoff-Relevant Information Architecture

This project studies how the design of information structures affects decisions, prices, and capital allocation when information is multidimensional and only some dimensions are payoff-relevant.

## Market for Credibility

This project develops a theory of credibility as a scarce resource in information markets.

## Proofs without Disclosure

This project studies strategic communication with privacy-preserving verifiable proofs.

## Firm Dynamics after IPO

This project examines how financial intermediary heterogeneity affects post-IPO growth, valuation, debt capacity, and upper-tail firm dynamics in Japan.

## Incremental Value of Data

This project studies the value of data through conditional mutual information and prediction-based decision problems.
